title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a team contribution and review protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

steps.title: Plan the evidence sequence

Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-integration-policy-ph6ix" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Integration Policy into observable performance evidence.

stimulus.title: Integration Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Integration Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Integration Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
